faq.question: Can foreigners open a company in Indonesia?
faq.answer: Yes. Foreigners can establish a PT PMA (foreign-owned company) in Indonesia.
faq.keywords: foreigners, non-Indonesians, PT PMA, start business

faq.question: What is a PT PMA?
faq.answer: A PT PMA is a foreign-owned company legally registered in Indonesia.

faq.question: How many shareholders are required?
faq.answer: Minimum two shareholders.

faq.question: What is the cost of company setup?
How long does company setup take?
faq.answer: About 5 working days.

faq.question: How many shareholders are required for a PMA company?
faq.answer: A minimum of two shareholders is required. Both shareholders can be foreigners.

faq.question: Can foreigners be directors of the company?
faq.answer: Yes. Foreign shareholders can act as Directors or Commissioners and sign company and bank documents.

faq.question: How long does it take to establish a PMA company?
faq.answer: The company setup usually takes 5 working days.

faq.question: What documents are needed to start a PMA company?
faq.answer: •	 Passport copies of two shareholders •	 Proposed company name (3 words) •	 Description of business activities

faq.question: What documents will the client receive after company setup?
faq.answer: •	 Company Deed (Akta Pendirian) •	 SK Kemenkumham (Legalization) •	 NIB (Business Identiﬁcation Number) •	 NPWP (Company Tax Number) •	 Business licenses (KBLI) •	 Company stamp •	 Soft copy and original hard copy documents

faq.question: Can company shareholders obtain a visa in Indonesia?
faq.answer: Yes. Shareholders can apply for a 2-year Investor Visa (KITAS Investor).

faq.question: What is the price of an investor visa?
faq.answer: IDR 18,500,000 per person.

faq.question: How long does the investor visa process take?
faq.answer: Around 5–7 working days after the company is established.

faq.question: What documents are required for the investor visa?
faq.answer: •	 Passport copy •	 Passport photo •	 Bank statement (minimum USD 2,000) •	 CV (Curriculum Vitae)

faq.question: Can the investor visa lead to permanent residency?
faq.answer: Yes. After several years, it may lead to a 5-year KITAP (permanent stay permit).

faq.question: How much does a PMA company setup cost?
faq.answer: IDR 30,000,000 (one-time fee).

faq.question: What does the company setup fee include?
faq.answer: •	 Notary services •	 Government registration •	 NIB & licenses •	 NPWP tax registration •	 Bank account assistance •	 Company documents

faq.question: Are there any additional beneﬁts included?
faq.answer: Yes. •	 3 months free virtual oﬃce •	 1 year free legal consultation •	 Support via WhatsApp and email

faq.question: Do companies need ongoing compliance services?
faq.answer: Yes. PMA companies must report taxes and submit regular reports.

faq.question: What services does Bali Legal Partner provide for maintenance?
faq.answer: •	 Monthly tax reporting •	 Accounting & bookkeeping •	 Payroll management •	 Annual tax reporting •	 LKPM investment reporting (quarterly)

faq.question: How much does ongoing compliance cost?
faq.answer: Starting from IDR 2,000,000 per month, depending on: •	 Number of invoices •	 Number of employees •	 Business activity volume

faq.question: What is KBLI?
faq.answer: KBLI is the Indonesian business classiﬁcation code used to deﬁne the business activities of a company.

faq.question: Who decides the KBLI code?
faq.answer: The client and Bali Legal Partner team decide together based on the business activity.

faq.question: Can foreigners operate villa rental businesses in Bali?
faq.answer: Yes, through a PMA company with proper licenses.

faq.question: What permits are required for villa rental?
faq.answer: Typical permits include: •	 IMB / PBG (Building Permit) •	 TDUP (Tourism Business License) •	 NIB (Business ID) •	 NPWPD (Local tax registration)

faq.question: Are there business sectors restricted for 100% foreign ownership?
faq.answer: Yes. Some sectors are restricted or closed. Examples include: •	 Wild capture ﬁshing •	 Small retail shops (warungs, kiosks) •	 Small-scale construction services •	 Local transportation services •	 Traditional media (TV, radio, newspapers)

faq.question: Can PMA companies do aquaculture?
faq.answer: Yes. Fish farming and aquaculture are allowed for PMA companies.

faq.question: Can PMA companies do wild ocean ﬁshing?
faq.answer: No. Wild capture ﬁshing in Indonesian waters is restricted to Indonesian companies.

faq.question: Can a PMA company rent property through Airbnb?
faq.answer: Yes, if the company has the correct KBLI codes and tourism licenses.

faq.question: Do companies need BPJS for employees?
faq.answer: Yes. Companies must register employees in: •	 BPJS Kesehatan (Health insurance) •	 BPJS Ketenagakerjaan (Employment insurance)

faq.question: What is KITAS?
faq.answer: KITAS is a 2-year investor visa that allows foreigners to live and work in Indonesia as a company shareholder. Cost is IDR 18,500,000.

faq.question: What is KITAP?
faq.answer: KITAP is a permanent stay permit. After several years on a KITAS investor visa, you may qualify for a 5-year KITAP.
